stimulus: For thousands of years, communities throughout the Andes ______blank the quinoa seed, a tiny nutritious grain, to make porridges, beverages, and ceremonial offerings. Curiously, archaeological evidence shows that quinoa cultivation spread northward into Mesoamerica only relatively recently, indicating a much later transfer than was once assumed.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. A main clause requires a finite verb. The present perfect “have harvested” correctly indicates an action that began in the past (“for thousands of years”) and continues into the present.

Choices A, B, and C are all nonfinite forms that cannot serve as the main verb of a clause.
